title: Test runner instability and cross-shell execution gaps (PowerShell + Docker)
description: Three recurring failures were observed in the Dockerized test flow: dependency prep (db + redis) failing intermittently, API functional tests failing with Prisma P1001 (db unreachable), and shell-specific test execution friction when `sh` was unavailable in PowerShell sessions.

errors.error: refresh scheduling test asserted cookie-sentinel payload `{}` but runtime used in-memory refresh token payload after login
errors.cause: After login, cookie mode still has an in-memory refresh token (`rt-1`), so scheduled refresh sends `{ refreshToken: "rt-1" }`, not `{}`. The old assertion was too broad and did not isolate refresh calls.
errors.fix: Updated the test to filter `/api/auth/refresh` calls and assert exactly one refresh call with `{ refreshToken: "rt-1" }`.

errors.error: db/redis test volumes collided across compose projects, causing MySQL lock failures (`Unable to lock ./ibdata1`) and failing health checks
errors.cause: Static volume names (`campusops-test-db-data`, `campusops-test-redis-data`, etc.) were shared by multiple compose project contexts, so old containers/metadata could keep files locked.
errors.fix: Parameterized test volume names with `${TEST_PROJECT_NAME:-campusops-test}-...` so test stacks can use isolated volume namespaces.

errors.error: test-runner could still reuse conflicting test-volume namespace when project metadata was stale
errors.cause: A fixed test namespace increased the chance of inheriting stale state from prior runs in mixed shell/compose contexts.
errors.fix: Set `TEST_PROJECT_NAME` to a unique per-run value (`${PROJECT_NAME}-${RANDOM}`) before compose execution, preventing cross-run volume lock conflicts.

errors.error: docs were stale relative to the latest fixes and shell behavior
errors.cause: Documentation still assumed `bash run_tests.sh` universally and referenced outdated migration wording in deployment docs.
errors.fix: Updated docs to use `./run_tests.sh`, added PowerShell Docker fallback commands, documented per-run test-volume isolation behavior, aligned deployment wording to current `prisma db push --accept-data-loss`, and corrected stale task path wording in top-level design docs.

rootCause: Two combined issues: (1) assertion drift vs actual auth refresh behavior in frontend tests, and (2) non-isolated Docker test volumes causing intermittent database lock/health failures that cascaded into API test connectivity errors; plus shell-specific command assumptions in docs.
impact: Intermittent or repeated red CI-style test summaries despite valid code, with false failure signals for db dependency prep and API suites; operator friction when running tests from PowerShell-only terminals.

validation.method: Dockerized test execution from PowerShell (no sh dependency)
validation.result: All suites passed in one consolidated run
